signals.reasoning: Live long is the priority. Unrealized P&L retained only 8% of peak ($0.37 vs $4.42), warningLevel is urgent, and this is classic momentum giveback after a squeeze. Even though funding remains deeply negative and the tape is still up on the day, position-management says protect capital and close the stale winner rather than let it round-trip under RED macro and veto.

Near-flat tape, negligible funding, very low squeeze/cascade risk, and no current position to manage. Under RED macro plus event veto, this is not a high-edge new entry or short; best action is to stand down.

Slightly weak relative tape, predicted funding turning positive, and neutral-to-soft mechanical bias, but no compelling asymmetry for a fresh short here. No live position and macro regime is hostile to forcing new exposure.

Mechanical board leans bearish with price down on the day and longs still paying funding, so directionally the short side is cleaner than the long side. Still, conviction stays moderate because there is no live position, the move is not extreme, and macro/veto say keep new risk small.

Strong squeeze-continuation mechanics with very negative funding and huge 24h price expansion, but execution quality is poor: only about $1.69M notional volume, wide 53 bps spread, and high crowding after a vertical move. Attractive on paper, but not appropriate to force as a new position in this consultation.

Bullish squeeze setup mechanically, but it is even thinner than TNSR with tiny notional volume, 67 bps spread, and high overcrowding/cascade risk. That is watchlist material, not something to add while the book already has a live management problem.

Still upward on the day and mechanically watch-worthy, but funding is not meaningfully supportive and the setup is not clean enough to justify fresh risk. With no live position here, the right move is patience.

Directionally bearish after a 13% down day, but the short is not crowded enough to give a clean positive-funding edge and much of the downside move already happened. Tradeable later if fresh rollover appears, but not the best use of risk right now.
Low-quality pop with mediocre liquidity, wide spread, and no strong mechanical edge either way. Easy pass under RED macro when there is no existing position to defend.

One of the cleaner bearish boards outside the live BLUR management issue: price is down sharply, funding is still positive, and open-interest-to-volume is elevated enough to support further unwind risk. Still only moderate confidence because this would be a new trade under veto conditions.

Bearish mechanical profile with price down, positive funding, decent liquidity, and tighter spread than most of the small-cap names. If the team insists on a fresh short after handling BLUR, this is one of the more orderly candidates, but BLUR management comes first.

Best fresh short candidate in this set after the live BLUR exit: bearish board, price down over 9%, positive funding, high OI/volume ratio, and the tightest spread in the alt basket. Even so, this is still secondary to flattening BLUR and should be sized defensively because macro regime is RED and event veto is active.
